channel.title: Claude Bar
channel.description: Auto-update feed for Claude Bar — menu-bar manager for multiple Claude Code accounts.

item.title: Version 10.34 (BETA)
item.description:
• Per-connector on/off toggle in Local MCP. Slack, ClickUp, GitHub, Google, and GitLab each get a switch — silence a provider's tools without deleting the token from your Keychain. Re-enable instantly when you need it back.
  • Local MCP tab reordered: connectors sit at the top, with Tool permissions and Gateway moved below so the controls you touch most are right under your cursor.

item.title: Version 10.33 (BETA)
item.description:
• Popover height now adapts to the number of accounts — 1 account is compact, 2 is mid, 3+ is full height with the list scrolling internally. Smoothly resizes when you add or remove an account.
  • Fixed ⌥X (Daily Briefing) firing on the wrong BriefingCoordinator instance. The hotkey now routes through a shared singleton so it always hits the live SwiftUI-owned coordinator.

item.title: Version 10.32 (BETA)
item.description:
• Header is now slim: status on the left, three evenly-spaced buttons on the right (Theme · Quit · Settings). Settings anchors the top-right corner.
  • Add account moved into Settings → General → "Accounts".
  • Verify all + Force refresh moved into Settings → Diagnostics, with inline outcome / cooldown state.
  • Briefing pill is gone — use ⌥X for Daily Briefing and ⌥Z to toggle the popover, same as before.

item.title: Version 10.31 (BETA)
item.description:
• Popover now scrolls when content runs long. Header status no longer gets clipped at the top, Token-usage KPI strip no longer cut off at the bottom on dense screens.
  • GitLab MCP add sheet redrawn — fixed-width label column, longer fields, larger window — so labels like "Personal Access Token" don't clip into the edge.
  • Fixed "Check for updates…" rendering on the popover instead of on Settings when invoked from About. The update overlay is mounted in both places now, so it lands centered on whichever window you're using.

item.title: Version 10.30 (BETA)
item.description:
• Alerts and floating sheets triggered from Settings now sit on top of the Settings window and centered on it — previously they could land behind Settings or off to one side.
  • Fixed /exit in claude-watch feeling like a freeze. Post-exit credential-change window shrank 8s → 2s, and the background session watcher is now SIGKILL'd on cleanup so the shell prompt actually returns.

item.title: Version 10.29 (BETA)
item.description:
• Footer is gone. Every global action (Add account, Verify all, Force refresh, Health check, Theme, Quit, Settings) lives in the header now. Settings anchors the top-right corner; Verify all is icon-only.
  • MCP tab drops the per-account override rows. Only the flat shared connectors list shows — no more six duplicate "using shared" entries below the canonical row.
  • Web-linked accounts now use WebView usage as source of truth. OAuth (the terminal-side /api/oauth/usage path) only fires when the web scrape returned absolutely nothing for that cycle.
  • Auto-swap threshold bar is taller, easier to grab, and stays a single accent blue instead of shifting through yellow/orange as you drag.
  • Fixed claude-watch refusing to let /exit when you had manually swapped accounts mid-session — the watcher now snapshots credentials at the moment claude exits and only auto-restarts on a real post-exit swap.

item.title: Version 10.28 (BETA)
item.description:
• Popover shrank to 440×720. Only what you actually need to see: header, accounts, auto-swap, footer.
  • Settings now opens as a real centered window with a left sidebar (App / System groups). It survives the popover collapsing and only closes when you actually close it.
  • MCP tab → GitLab → Connect now opens the multi-instance Add sheet directly, instead of falling back to the generic single-token paste flow.
  • Fixed ⌥Z and ⌥X hotkeys not firing on a cold-launched session — registration now happens at app launch instead of waiting for the popover's first open.

item.title: Version 10.27 (BETA)
item.description:
• MCP Cancel now actually dismisses the connect window. When the menu-bar popover collapses behind the floating sheet, Cancel used to clear coordinator state but leave the window on screen. The sheet now closes the window directly.
  • Briefing pill pinned to true center. No more drift when the status text on the left grows or shrinks.

item.title: Version 10.26 (BETA)
item.description: • Briefing entry moved to the header. The Daily Briefing button now sits as a centered pill in the popover header instead of in the footer — fewer footer icons, more prominent placement.

item.title: Version 10.25 (BETA)
item.description:
• Swap no longer asks you to re-login on a transient OAuth blip. Rate-limit (429) and network errors during refresh now defer the swap with a clear retry hint instead of telling you to re-authenticate.
  • Polished failure card. When a swap can't complete, you get an elegant overlay with icon, plain-language explanation, and a one-click Retry or Re-login button.
  • Backup credential safety. Keychain ACL migration retries its re-add step, so a transient Keychain hiccup can no longer orphan a backup blob.
  • ~/.claude.json read race fix. Swap absorbs the partial-write race when Claude Code rewrites the config concurrently.
  • 30s swap timeout. No more hanging on a stale lock — fails fast with an actionable message.
  • Footer drops the Briefing button (Daily Briefing still opens via ⌥X).

item.title: Version 10.24 (BETA)
item.description: • Removed the empty space below the Today / This week / This month strip. The token-usage chart now grows to fill the popover so the KPI strip sits flush above the footer regardless of how many accounts you have.

item.title: Version 10.23 (BETA)
item.description: • Settings now uses a top tab strip instead of the left sidebar. General, MCP, Briefing, Privacy, Diagnostics, and About sit as inline chips across the top of the Settings overlay, giving each detail pane the full modal width.

item.title: Version 10.22 (BETA)
item.description:
• Popover redesign — no more top tab strip. Opening Claude Bar lands you straight on a single scrollable surface: account list (3 visible, scroll past), Auto-swap controls, then the token-usage chart with Today / Week / Month totals. No more bouncing between Dashboard, Accounts, Stats, and Settings tabs to find anything.
  • Footer adds Add account and Settings buttons. Add account opens the bilingual EN/VI guidance card with an "Open Terminal & run claude /login" action; Settings opens the sidebar (General, MCP, Briefing, Privacy, Diagnostics, About) as a modal overlay dismissible with Esc or by clicking outside.
  • Account list scrolls past 3 accounts (was 6) so the chart stays visible even with several accounts added.
  • "Verify all", per-row Switch, and the ⋯ context menu keep their existing behaviour.

item.title: Version 10.21 (BETA)
item.description:
• Hotfix — second macOS Keychain prompt on update is gone. v10.19 blocked the iCloud-sync passphrase prompt; this build also blocks the claude.ai web profile prompt (one Keychain item per linked account email). Combined, every Sparkle update now lands with zero ACL prompts unless you flip the toggle on yourself.
  • What's new. The Diagnostics "Enable iCloud sync" toggle now also gates the web profile Keychain cache. Same toggle, broader scope: when off, Claude Bar never reads any Keychain item.
  • Known issue. When the toggle is off, claude.ai web profiles still work (cookies live in WKWebsiteDataStore on disk) but won't be restored from the Keychain cache. If a web profile fails to load after upgrade, flip the toggle on once and restart polling.

item.title: Version 10.20 (BETA)
item.description:
• Menu-bar popover collapsed from 8 tabs to 4. The old layout crammed Accounts, General, MCP, Claude, Daily, Diagnostics, Privacy, and About across the top — with the "Claude" tab mixing the account list, the auto-swap toggle, and the token-usage chart on one screen. v10.20 collapses those eight into Dashboard, Accounts, Stats, and Settings.
  • New Dashboard is now the default landing tab. Active account at a glance with a one-click switch dropdown, plus Today / Week / Month token totals.
  • Auto-swap controls moved into the Accounts tab — next to the list they actually affect, instead of buried under "Claude".
  • Settings is one home for everything configurable: General, MCP, Briefing, Privacy, Diagnostics, and About all live behind a single sidebar.
  • Footer "Daily" button renamed to "Briefing" so it no longer collides visually with the Settings sub-tab of the same name.

item.title: Version 10.19 (BETA)
item.description:
• What's new — iCloud sync toggle now resets to OFF on every Sparkle update. Each new build lands on a clean default-off, so the macOS Keychain prompt only ever appears when you explicitly opt in to sync for that release. Within the same version the toggle keeps your choice across launches; it only resets when the version string changes.
  • Hotfix. Replaced the v10.18 one-shot force-off with a per-version reset, so the default-off promise holds for every future update — not just the upgrade from v10.16.
  • Known issue. If you sync across Macs, re-enable the toggle in Diagnostics → iCloud Sync once after every update. Your bundle and passphrase remain intact in iCloud between updates.

item.title: Version 10.18 (BETA)
item.description:
• Hotfix — iCloud sync toggle now actually defaults off. v10.17 documented default-off, but a stale UserDefaults value written by v10.16's auto-migration kept the toggle stuck on for upgraders. This build runs a one-shot reset so the toggle reads off after upgrading, matching the documented behavior.
  • Known issue. If you were syncing on v10.16 or earlier, re-enable the toggle once in Diagnostics → iCloud Sync to resume cross-Mac sync. Your bundle and passphrase remain intact in iCloud.

item.title: Version 10.17 (BETA)
item.description:
• What's new — About panel now shows per-release notes inline. Open About → Claude Bar to read what's new, hotfixes, and known issues for the version you're running. The channel badge follows the version: bare-integer = Stable (green), dotted = Beta (orange).
  • iCloud sync defaults to OFF for everyone. The v10.16 migration that silently auto-enabled sync for users with an existing bundle is gone — the toggle in Diagnostics → iCloud Sync is now off after upgrade, even for prior sync users. Flip it back on to resume cross-Mac sync.
  • Hotfix — Command Center loads cleanly with no SSH hosts / GitLab instances. Empty lists no longer surface a red "decodingFailed: found null" error in the Diagnostics tab.
  • Known issue. Existing iCloud sync users will see the toggle off after this update — your bundle and passphrase are untouched in iCloud, just re-enable the toggle to resume.

item.title: Version 10.16 (BETA)
item.description:
• BETA RELEASE — not stable. Wide change surface this cycle (20 new MCP tools across 6 services + a Keychain-ACL prompt fix that touches launch-time code paths). If you depend on Claude Bar for daily account-switch work, stay on v10.15 until the next stable spin.
  • Updates no longer trigger a macOS "Allow ClaudeBar to access this keychain item?" prompt every time you upgrade. iCloud sync is now an explicit toggle in Diagnostics (off by default). Users who were already syncing get auto-migrated on first launch — your existing bundle and passphrase survive. Turning the toggle off doesn't delete anything; it just stops Claude Bar reading the Keychain until you opt back in.
  • GitLab MCP grew from 7 to 19 tools, finally reaching parity with the GitHub surface. New reads: MR diff, MR / issue notes, MR changes, get_issue, get_file (raw text at a ref), list_branches, list_commits, list_pipelines. New gated writes: create_issue, create_mr (draft-aware), update_issue.
  • Google Workspace MCP filled out: Drive gained list_folder + download_file (raw bytes for non-Doc files), Calendar gained list_calendars + get_free_busy, Gmail gained list_labels + get_thread (full conversation with every message expanded).
  • SSH MCP added read_file — head -c with a server-side byte cap (default 100 KiB, max 5 MiB). Safer than exec("cat") when the agent just needs a config file's contents.
  • Bitwarden MCP added list_folders so the agent can discover folder IDs before constraining a vault search, instead of asking you for them.

item.title: Version 10.15
item.description:
• Connect window now shows a green "Successfully connected" confirmation for 1.2 seconds before auto-closing, so you can see the MCP actually wired up instead of the panel just vanishing. Applies to every connector — Slack, ClickUp, GitHub, and Google.
  • GitHub MCP grew from 9 to 28 tools. Reads now cover the missing single-gets (issue, commit, file content at a ref), PR review threads and inline comments, files / commits in a PR, branches, commit history, issue search, CI check-runs and Actions workflow runs. Writes add create_issue, create_pr (draft-aware), update_issue (title/body/labels/assignees/milestone), request_reviewers, add_labels, and remove_label — all surface a confirm gate before hitting GitHub.
  • Slack MCP went from 3 to 9 tools. New reads: channel history (so the agent can see what is happening in a channel right now, not just search hits), user list, single user lookup, and message permalink. New gated writes: post_message and reply_thread.
  • ClickUp MCP added 4 tools. Reads: list_comments (closes the gap where the agent could not read comment bodies), list_members (resolve assignee IDs to names), and search_tasks (cross-workspace filter without iterating list-by-list). Write: update_task to edit name / description / priority / due / start, alongside the existing update_task_status.

item.title: Version 10.14
item.description:
• GitHub MCP connector now supports a personal access token (PAT). Open Settings → Local MCP, click Connect on the GitHub row, paste a token from github.com/settings/tokens — no OAuth app setup required. Both classic (ghp_…) and fine-grained (github_pat_…) tokens are accepted.
  • Fixed alerts and file pickers (rename account, cloud passphrase, avatar pick, repo link, chat attachment, diagnostics export/import, swap confirm) appearing behind the menu-bar popover or jumping to a different Space.

item.title: Version 10.13
item.description:
• Connect failures in the MCP connector window now surface inline with a red error banner and progress indicator, instead of silently doing nothing while the popover hid the result.
  • Added orange format hints when the pasted token / client ID does not match the expected prefix (Slack xoxp-/xoxe-/xoxb-, ClickUp pk_, Google .apps.googleusercontent.com) so typos are caught before the round-trip.

item.title: Version 10.12
item.description:
• MCP connector inputs (Slack, ClickUp, Google) no longer collapse the menu-bar popover when you click into a field.
  • Silenced automatic "new version available" notifications — check manually from About when you want them.

item.title: Version 10.11
item.description:
• Auto-resume Claude inside cmux terminal panes after each account swap so the conversation continues under the new account — no toggle required.
  • Panes pinning an isolated CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR (for example ~/.codex-accounts/claude/<id>/) are intentionally skipped; claude-bar's global swap does not reach them.

item.title: Version 10.10
item.description:
• Fixed cross-device Claude web cookie sync so two Macs no longer overwrite each other's fresh session and silently log you out.
  • Restore now picks the most recent cookies between local Keychain and the iCloud bundle instead of always trusting local.
  • Expired cookies are filtered on save and restore, and SameSite is preserved across sync round-trips.

item.title: Version 10.9
item.description:
• New command-center UI with session panel, capture box, servers rail, scheduler settings, and notifications.
  • MCP integrations for GitLab, ClickUp capture, and Bitwarden, plus write tools and audit hardening.
  • SSH server manager with ControlMaster, age-encrypted bundles, and tail/exec from MCP.
  • Destructive action gate with widget UI and UDS IPC bridge.
  • Popover destructive-gate sheet now dismisses correctly after MCP connect.

item.title: Version 10.8
item.description: • 'Check for updates…' no longer dismisses the popover. The whole update flow — checking, update found, download, install — now renders as an overlay centred on the popover, and the popover only closes on the usual outside-click.

item.title: Version 10.7
item.description:
• Daily window opens and closes faster — animation tightened so the resize no longer feels like it lags behind the click.
  • Daily and the menu-bar popover now auto-dismiss the moment you switch to another app (Chrome, Xcode, etc.), so they stop covering your real workspace.

item.title: Version 10.6
item.description: • Fixed popover opening on the wrong display in extended/duplicate setups — it now follows the cursor's screen instead of always sticking to the menu-bar status item's screen.

item.title: Version 10.5
item.description: • Fixed Daily window getting orphaned and unclosable after rapid ⌥X hotkey toggles — the X button and hotkey now reliably close it.

item.title: Version 10.4
item.description:
• Welcome onboarding now scrolls and surfaces the bilingual add-account guidance card up front, so first-time users see the /logout explainer before adding their first account.
  • Onboarding window grew from 460 → 620 px so the new card fits comfortably without forcing a scroll on standard displays.

item.title: Version 10.3
item.description:
• Daily background update checks are now enabled.
  • Stale "no public release pipeline" comment removed.
  • packaging/appcast.xml committed on main as feed source-of-truth.

item.title: Version 10.2
item.description:
• First release served through the Sparkle auto-update pipeline.
  • Sparkle EdDSA signing live; appcast hosted at the SUFeedURL.
  • Real public key embedded; signed zip enclosure.
